journey.name: Baby growth percentile — signup to history & chart
journey.description: Sign up a fresh account, choose the baby scale, add a baby with a known sex + birthdate, add a manual baby weight entry, then confirm the growth percentile shows on the history list, the day-detail rows, and the dashboard chart marker.

actions.action: On the landing screen, tap "Sign Up" (create a new account).
Enter a unique email (e.g. ggtest+<timestamp>@gg.co) and a password (123456), then continue.
Complete any required profile fields to finish signup and reach the home/dashboard.
Open the add-device / scale flow and select the Baby Scale ("Smart Baby").
Choose "Add a baby" / skip device pairing if prompted (no hardware needed).
Add a baby: name "Test Baby", sex "Male", birthdate 8 weeks ago (note the exact date).
Save the baby profile and return to the baby dashboard.
Open manual entry for the baby and add a WEIGHT reading near the 50th percentile for an 8-week-old boy (about 12.5 lb / ~5.7 kg). Save it.
Verify the entry appears on the baby dashboard / latest reading.
Navigate to History for the baby.
Verify the history week row shows a percentile value (NOT "--") next to weight/length, e.g. a number around 50 with the "th" unit.
Tap the week row to open the day detail.
Verify the day-detail entry row shows the same percentile value (NOT "--").
Add a second weight entry clearly low (about 9 lb) and verify its percentile is much lower than the first.
Open the dashboard growth chart and verify the percentile band curves are drawn and the curve/axis starts at the baby's birthdate.
Tap a point on the chart and verify the focus label shows a percentile number.
